subject: Accounting
title: Special Journals
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Special Journals.

hook.text: A busy store processes 500 sales a day. Recording each one in the general journal would be chaos.

buildup.text: Special journals handle high-volume, repetitive transactions—sales, purchases, cash receipts, cash payments.

discovery.text: The sales journal records only credit sales. The cash receipts journal records only cash inflows.

twist.text: At the end of the period, totals from special journals are posted to the general ledger in one batch.

climax.text: This saves time, reduces errors, and keeps the general journal clean for unusual transactions.

punchline.text: Routine transactions get their own lane. Speed and accuracy follow.

quiz.question: Why are special journals used?
options: To handle high-volume repetitive transactions efficiently
